proof: function(x0: Nat, x1: Nat, x2: Nat) { x0.suc != x1 or (if x1.divides(x2) { List.cons(x1, divisors_up_to(x2, x0)) } else { divisors_up_to(x2, x0) }) = divisors_up_to(x2, x1) }(k, k.suc, n)
function(x0: Nat, x1: Nat, x2: Nat) { not x0.divides(x1) or x2.suc != x0 or List.cons(x0, divisors_up_to(x1, x2)) = divisors_up_to(x1, x0) }(k.suc, n, k)
function(x0: Nat, x1: Nat, x2: Nat) { x0.suc != x1 or (if x1.divides(x2) { List.cons(x1, divisors_up_to(x2, x0)) } else { divisors_up_to(x2, x0) }) = divisors_up_to(x2, x1) }(k, k.suc, n)
function(x0: Nat, x1: Nat, x2: Nat) { x0.suc != x1 or divisors_up_to(x2, x0) = divisors_up_to(x2, x1) or x1.divides(x2) }(k, k.suc, n)
function(x0: Nat, x1: Nat) { Nat.zero != x0 or List.nil[Nat] = divisors_up_to(x1, x0) }(0, n)
function(x0: Nat, x1: Nat -> Nat) { sum[Nat](map[Nat, Nat](divisor_list(x0), x1)) = divisor_sum_fn(x1, x0) }(n, f)
